name: Wild Rice Crepes
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend the egg replacer with 1/2 cup water until smooth. Add remaining ingredients and blend one minute at high speed.
2. Heat a crepe pan or non-stick skillet over low heat, brush the bottom lightly with oil (I use unrefined corn oil). When heated, take off the burner, let cool about three seconds, then pour 3-4 tbsp batter into it. Swirl the pan so the bottom is coated with a thin layer of batter. Put back on the burner and cook until lightly browned. Flip the crepe and cook until that side is also lightly browned. Repeat with remaining batter. This makes about a dozen crepes.
3. For stuffing: Sauté onion in a little dry white wine for a minute or two, then add wild rice and stock, cover tightly, bring to a boil, turn heat to low and cook about an hour until all liquid is absorbed.
4. While the rice cooks, sauté celery in oil until slightly tender, add onions and all mushrooms, and sauté until all vegetables are medium tender. Add cooked rice, tomato, and seasoning, then cook another ten minutes or so. Fill crepes with the mixture, roll them over it, and heat in a preheated oven at 350°F for about ten minutes.
5. Serve with Nutty Almond Sauce and garnish with parsley and a twisted orange slice.
For Nutty Almond Sauce:
- Unrefined corn oil 3 tbsp
- Unbleached flour 4 tbsp
- Soy milk, heated 2 1/2 cups
- Chopped onion leaves 1/2 cup
- Garlic cloves 4
- White pepper to taste
- Freshly grated nutmeg a dash
- Salt to taste
